rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ADVENTURE INVESTMENTS SF LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property / Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 4, 2026, line 1. Petition to Release Property from Lien is OFF CALENDAR. Dismissal on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS ANDERSON ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property / Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 4, 2026, line 3. Defendant's Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint is continued to April 17, 2026. No later than April 1, 2026 Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration (1) stating the date of death of each Plaintiff listed in the request for dismissal filed on February 18, 2026; (2) listing all allegations added to Second Amended Complaint specifically referencing each dismissed Plaintiff. (e.g. as to Plaintiff Matthew Byrd the following allegations have been added: paragraph number and text of the paragraph); (3) as to each allegation pertaining to each dismissed Plaintiff, counsel to provide a date when the facts/information regarding these allegations have been obtained from each dismissed Plaintiff; if information was not obtained from each dismissed Plaintiff directly, provide date and name of the person supplying the facts to counsel; (4) for each dismissed Plaintiff provide the date counsel was retained. Plaintiff shall also file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) providing authority allowing counsel to dismiss a case on behalf of a decedent. No later than April 1, 2026 counsel for Defendant to file a declaration and a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) discussing the legal significance of Declaration of Christina Forst filed on January 21, 2026. Specifically, (1) is it Defendant's contention that this filing served as an automatic extension to respond; or (2) if it was not intended to serve as an automatic extension, did the Defendant obtain a court order for extension. If the filing was intended as an automatic extension, Defendant shall cite authority allowing for such extension. If the filing was not intended as an automatic extension provide authority as to the legal significance of this filing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAHIRA VIERA ET AL VS. 735 O' FARRELL I7, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relif Under C.C.P. Section 473(B) And/Or To Withdraw Answer On Behalf Of Defendant Dexter Cato; Declaration Of Justin M. Marvisi
rulings.Rulings: Real Property / Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 4, 2026, line 4. Defendant's Motion for Relief under CCP 473(b) and to Withdraw Answer on Behalf of Defendant Dexter Cato is continued to March 13, 2026. No later than March 9, 2026 parties to file supplemental briefs (not to exceed 5 pages) addressing the following issues: (1) does a filing of a pleading by counsel without permission of a party constitute general appearance or any appearance; (2) discussing case authorities re: applicability of CCP 473(b) (See Notice of Motion) and/or CCP 473(a) (See Points and Authorities) to the facts of this case. If no such cases are found, state "none" in the brief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
